evals.name: plan-review-refactor
evals.prompt: I just finished a plan to refactor the reward function in our RL training system. The plan has 5 steps: extract reward_tool_strict into two functions, update the config YAML to point to the new functions, add unit tests, run the existing test suite, and update the docs. Can you get a second opinion from Codex on this plan?
evals.expected_output: Should detect plan review mode, send to Codex with plan-specific dimensions (goal alignment, completeness, risks, ordering, testability), present Codex feedback, and iterate if needed

evals.name: code-review-staged-diff
evals.prompt: I've staged some changes to the dataset loader — added retry logic for failed API calls and a new config parameter. /codex-review
evals.expected_output: Should detect code review mode via git diff --cached, send to Codex with code-specific dimensions (correctness, security, edge cases, error handling, compatibility, test coverage), show proposed fixes if any

evals.name: explicit-model-override
evals.expected_output: Should detect available content (plan or diff), use gpt-5.4 instead of default gpt-5.3-codex, and proceed with the appropriate review mode
